cropDiseases.symptoms: Yellow linear pustules on leaves, reduced vigour, shrivelled grains.
cropDiseases.cause: Fungal infection favoured by cool and humid weather.
cropDiseases.treatment: Spray a recommended triazole fungicide at first appearance and repeat based on label guidance.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties, balanced nitrogen, and avoid dense late crop.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Earheads replaced by black powdery spores.
cropDiseases.treatment: There is no curative treatment after emergence.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use treated certified seed and resistant varieties.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Spindle-shaped lesions on leaves and neck rot on panicles.
cropDiseases.cause: Magnaporthe infection under humidity and excess nitrogen.
cropDiseases.treatment: Spray a recommended fungicide and manage standing water carefully.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen, and maintain spacing.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing from leaf tips with wavy margins and drying leaves.
cropDiseases.cause: Bacterial infection spread by rain splash and wounds.
cropDiseases.treatment: Use copper-based bactericide only where recommended locally and avoid field-to-field spread.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use clean seed, balanced fertilization, and avoid excess nitrogen.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Brown circular spots with concentric rings on older leaves.
cropDiseases.treatment: Remove heavily infected debris and spray a recommended fungicide if severe.
cropDiseases.prevention: Crop rotation, residue management, and balanced nutrition.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Angular leaf spots, black arm on stems, boll lesions.
cropDiseases.treatment: There is limited curative control; focus on sanitation and local advisory.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use acid-delinted treated seed and tolerant varieties.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Target-like concentric spots on older leaves and stem lesions.
cropDiseases.cause: Alternaria fungus favoured by warm humidity.
cropDiseases.treatment: Spray a recommended protectant or systemic fungicide and remove affected leaves.
cropDiseases.prevention: Mulching, lower leaf pruning, crop rotation, and drip irrigation.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions, white growth under humid conditions, fruit rot.
cropDiseases.cause: Phytophthora infection during cool wet spells.
cropDiseases.treatment: Immediate fungicide spray and removal of infected plants where severe.
cropDiseases.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation and ensure good airflow.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Dark lesions on leaves and stems with rapid field spread.
cropDiseases.cause: Phytophthora infestans in cool wet weather.
cropDiseases.treatment: Protective and systemic fungicides as per local recommendation.
cropDiseases.prevention: Use healthy seed tubers and prophylactic sprays in conducive weather.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Brown lesions with concentric rings and premature defoliation.
cropDiseases.treatment: Timely fungicide spray and improved plant nutrition.
cropDiseases.prevention: Crop rotation and residue destruction.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Tiny brown pustules on leaf underside and rapid yellowing.
cropDiseases.treatment: Spray a recommended fungicide early after detection.
cropDiseases.prevention: Regular scouting and timely sowing.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Dark sunken lesions on stems and pods, poor seed quality.
cropDiseases.treatment: Seed treatment and field fungicide where advised.
cropDiseases.prevention: Disease-free seed and crop rotation.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Long cigar-shaped grey lesions on leaves.
cropDiseases.cause: Exserohilum fungal infection under cool humid conditions.
cropDiseases.treatment: Fungicide spray if disease appears before tasseling and pressure is high.
cropDiseases.prevention: Resistant hybrids and residue management.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Small rectangular brown lesions that expand and merge.
cropDiseases.treatment: Timely fungicide under severe incidence.
cropDiseases.prevention: Hybrid selection and field sanitation.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Upward curling leaves, stunting, poor fruit set.
cropDiseases.treatment: Rouge infected plants early and manage whitefly vector.
cropDiseases.prevention: Yellow sticky traps, barrier crops, and vector control.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Dark spots on leaves, stems, and siliquae.
cropDiseases.treatment: Recommended fungicide sprays during humid spells.
cropDiseases.prevention: Seed treatment and wider spacing.

cropDiseases.symptoms: White blister-like pustules on underside of leaves and staghead.
cropDiseases.treatment: Fungicide if early incidence is observed.
cropDiseases.prevention: Crop rotation and clean seed.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Sudden drooping and yellowing with vascular discoloration.
cropDiseases.treatment: No effective rescue after severe wilt.
cropDiseases.prevention: Resistant varieties, seed treatment, and long rotation.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Circular lesions on leaves, stems, and pods.
cropDiseases.treatment: Protective fungicide after first signs in cool humid periods.
cropDiseases.prevention: Disease-free seed and field sanitation.

cropDiseases.symptoms: Yellow streaks turning into elongated brown spots.
cropDiseases.treatment: Remove old leaves and spray a recommended fungicide.
cropDiseases.prevention: Good aeration, sanitation, and balanced potassium.

pests.name: Fall Armyworm
pests.symptoms: Windowing on leaves, whorl feeding, frass in funnel.
pests.management: Scout early, destroy egg masses, encourage natural enemies, and use recommended insecticide only when threshold is crossed.

pests.name: Brown Planthopper
pests.symptoms: Hopper burn patches, insects at plant base.
pests.management: Avoid excess nitrogen, drain field temporarily, and use targeted insecticide if infestation is high.

pests.name: Pink Bollworm
pests.symptoms: Rosette flowers, damaged bolls, larvae inside locules.
pests.management: Timely sowing, pheromone traps, crop residue destruction, and advised insecticide rotation.

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.symptoms: Leaf yellowing, honeydew, sooty mould, virus spread.
pests.management: Yellow sticky traps, avoid overuse of nitrogen, and rotate insecticides by mode of action.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.symptoms: Curling leaves, sticky honeydew, weak growth.
pests.management: Conserve ladybird beetles, water spray on light attack, and use a selective insecticide if severe.

pests.name: Jassid
pests.symptoms: Leaf edge yellowing and hopper burn.
pests.management: Seed treatment, tolerant varieties, and threshold-based spray.

pests.name: Thrips
pests.symptoms: Silvery streaks, leaf curling, reduced vigour.
pests.management: Blue sticky traps, avoid dusty stress, and rotate insecticides carefully.

pests.name: Helicoverpa Armigera
pests.symptoms: Pod and fruit boring with round holes and excreta.
pests.management: Pheromone traps, bird perches, NPV or Bt-based options, and threshold-based spray.

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.management: Clip seedling tips where applicable, destroy stubbles, and apply granules only as recommended.

pests.name: Mealybug
pests.management: Destroy alternate hosts, use sticky barriers, and encourage predators.

fertilizers.guide: Typical balanced plan uses basal phosphorus and potassium with split nitrogen at sowing, CRI, and later growth stage based on soil test.
Basal phosphorus and potassium with split nitrogen across transplanting, tillering, and panicle initiation, adjusted by water and variety.
Apply phosphorus and potash near sowing and split nitrogen through square and boll development. Avoid excessive nitrogen.
Use compost or FYM before planting, then crop-specific NPK through basal and fertigation or side-dressings.
Always prefer soil-test based doses. Organic sources like FYM, vermicompost, neem cake, and green manuring improve soil health.

# Biostadt Biostimulant & Fertilizer Guide

Complete reference for Biostadt's seaweed-based biostimulants, specialty biostimulants, and micronutrient fertilizers.

---

## BIOZYME PRODUCT FAMILY

All BIOZYME products are derived from **Atlantic Seaweed (Ascophyllum nodosum)** using **B.I.L.T. (Biostadt Induced Lacto-fermentation Technology)**, which preserves the full spectrum of bioactive compounds.

### Core Benefits Across All BIOZYME Products
- Primary & Secondary (feeder) root development
- Increases Microbial Activity in soil and nutrient uptake
- Develops tolerance for biotic & abiotic stress
- Improves photosynthesis and the greenness of plants
- Enhances bud stimulation for more fruiting/productive tillers

---

## BIOZYME GRANULES — Crop-Specific Selection Guide

| Product | Target Crop | Key Feature | Dose |
|---|---|---|---|
| BIOZYME GRANULES | All crops | General root & plant development | 8-16 kg/acre |
| BIOZYME POWER+ GRANULES | Paddy | Productive tillers | 6-12 kg/acre |
| BIOZYME BIOCANE+ GRANULES | Sugarcane | Tillering, inter nodal length, cane girth | 8-16 kg/acre |
| BIOZYME FRUIT+ GRANULES | All Fruit crops | Root development & bud stimulation | Apple: 750 gm/plant; Others: 48-60 kg/acre |
| BIOZYME VEGETABLES+ GRANULES | All Vegetables | Root & plant development | 8-16 kg/acre |
| BIOZYME GRAIN+ GRANULES | Wheat, Maize, Bajra | More productive tillers | 8-16 kg/acre |
| AMAZE X | All crops | Silica-based triple action formula | 4-8 kg/acre |

---

## BIOZYME LIQUID — Crop-Specific Selection Guide

| Product | Target Crop | Application Stage | Dose |
|---|---|---|---|
| BIOZYME CROP+ | All Crops | Vegetative stage | 250-300 ml/acre |
| BIOZYME POWER+ | Paddy | Panicle initiation | 250-300 ml/acre |
| BIOZYME SEED+ | All Crops | Seed treatment / Seedling | 3-5 ml/kg seed |
| BIOZYME DRIP+ | All Crops (drip) | Active growing season | 2.5 Lit/Ha |
| BIOZYME FRUIT+ | All Fruit Crops | Fruit development | 1.5-2 ml/lit water |
| BIOZYME VEGETABLE+ | All Vegetables | Flowering to harvest | 250-300 ml/acre |
| BIOZYME PULSE+ | Soybean, Groundnut, Pulses | Flowering & pod filling | 250-300 ml/acre |
| BIOZYME MIRCHI+ | Chilli | Flowering & fruit development | 250-300 ml/acre |
| BIOZYME TEA+ | Tea | Multiple pickings | 500 ml/Ha |
| BIOZYME BT+ | Cotton | Flowering & boll development | 250-300 ml/acre |

---

## SPECIALTY BIOSTIMULANTS

### AMAZE XL — Premium Amino Acid + Seaweed
- **Content:** Seaweed (A. nodosum) 8% + L-Amino acids 20%
- **Unique:** Combination of Korean Amino Acids (microbial fermentation) + Norwegian Seaweed (BILT)
- **Amino acids:** All in free L-Form (natural available form); six types of high purity; Low Chlorine & Na
- **Best use:** During Flowers/Fruit Development stage
- **Benefits:** Improves fruit setting, reduces fruit drop, uniform shape/size/color, improves weight & TSS
- **Dose:** 1-2 ml/lit water; Crops: All fruits & vegetables

### REJOICE WG — First WG Formulation Biostimulant
- **Content:** Seaweed extract 8% + Hydrolyzed proteins 5% + Polysaccharides 25%
- **Unique:** First Wettable Granule (WG) formulation in India; FFS (Flowering and Fruiting Stimulation) technology
- **Benefits:** More flowers, reduces flower drop, improves fruit setting, avoids early abortion of bunches
- **Dose:** 1-2 gm/lit water or 200 gm/acre; All crops

---

## BIOGREEN MICRONUTRIENT FERTILIZERS

All BioGreen products use **European microencapsulation technology** for superior quality and maximum bioavailability.

### Application Timing Guide

#### BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS (Zinc Oxide 39.5% suspension)
Zinc is essential for: Enzyme activity in photosynthesis, root development, disease immunity

| Crop | Application Timing |
|---|---|
| Fruit crops | 30-35 days and 45-50 days after pruning/bahar treatment |
| Vegetables | 20-25 days and 55-60 days after transplanting |
| Field crops | 25-30 days after sowing |
| Cumin | 30-35 days after sowing |
| Gram | 30-35 days after sowing and 90 days after planting |

**Dose:** 0.5-1 ml/lit water

#### BIOGREEN BORON (Boron Ethanolamine 10%)
Boron is essential for: Sugar translocation, pollination, seed setting

| Crop | Application Timing |
|---|---|
| Fruit crops | At pre-flowering stage + At fruit setting stage |
| Vegetables | At Flowering stage; repeat after every 2 pickings |
| Field crops | Before flowering stage |
| Melons | At flowering stage + Fruit setting stage |

**Dose:** 1-1.5 ml/lit water

#### BIOGREEN CALCIUM (Concentrated Liquid Calcium 11%)
Calcium is essential for: Cell wall strengthening, stress management, fruit quality

| Crop | Application Timing |
|---|---|
| Fruit crops | 60, 40, and 20 days before harvest |
| Vegetables | 40-50 days after fruit development; 5-6 days before harvesting |
| Melons | Fruit development stage |

**Dose:** 2-3 ml/lit water

#### BIOGREEN CALCIUM B PLUS (Ca 14% + B 6.9% + N 4%)
Combined Calcium & Boron — reduces number of applications

| Crop | Application Timing |
|---|---|
| Fruit crops | Pre-flowering; 30, 60, 75 days after fruit setting |
| Vegetables | Pre-flowering; after 2 pickings; every 15-20 days till last harvest |

**Dose:** 1 ml/lit water

#### BIOGREEN POTASH (Concentrated Liquid Potassium 46.5%)
Potassium is essential for: Strong growth, fruit quality, uniform size/color, faster ripening

| Crop | Application Timing |
|---|---|
| Fruit crops | 30 and 15 days before harvesting |
| Vegetables | One week before harvesting; repeat after every 2 pickings |
| Field crops | 60-65 days after sowing |
| Cumin | At seed setting + 15 days before harvesting |

**Dose:** 2-2.5 ml/lit water

#### BIOGREEN ZINC — Chelated (Zinc EDTA 12%)
Water soluble; can be applied foliar or through drip/soil

| Crop Type | Foliar Dose | Soil/Drip Dose |
|---|---|---|
| Fruits (Grapes, Pomegranate, Citrus, Banana) | 1.5-2 gm/liter; 2-3 applications | 2.5-5 kg/ha; 2-3 applications |
| Vegetables (Chilli, Tomato, Onion, Brinjal) | 1-1.5 gm/liter; 2 applications | Through drip with WSF |
| Field Crops (Cotton, Rice, Sugarcane) | 1-1.5 gm/liter; 2-3 applications | Through drip with WSF |

#### BIOGREEN IRON — Chelated (Ferrous EDTA 12%)
Essential for: Chlorophyll production, photosynthesis, nitrogen fixation, protein synthesis
Same dosage structure as BIOGREEN ZINC (chelated)

---

## MOBILIZER — Mycorrhizal Biofertilizer

### What It Does
MOBILIZER contains over 90% Arbuscular Mycorrhiza fungus spores using Sol-Gel technology:
- Extends root absorption area by **more than 700 times**
- Functions as root extension to access water & nutrients beyond the Root Zone
- Hyphae absorbs and actively delivers nutrients directly to roots
- Symbiosis increases effective water utilization
- Improves tolerance to stress and resistance to drought

### How It Works
Spores are harvested from fungal culture, made into Gel, diluted with bioavailable nutrients, and coated on Bentonite for better absorption and durability.

---

## COMPLETE NUTRITION PROGRAM

### Stage-Wise Application for Fruit Crops
1. **Pre-planting / Soil preparation:** MOBILIZER (mycorrhizal) + AMAZE X granules
2. **Vegetative stage:** BIOZYME FRUIT+ GRANULES (soil) + BIOZYME CROP+ (foliar) + BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS
3. **Pre-flowering:** BIOGREEN BORON + BIOGREEN CALCIUM B PLUS + REJOICE WG or AMAZE XL
4. **Fruit development:** BIOZYME FRUIT+ (foliar) + BIOGREEN CALCIUM (3 times till harvest) + AMAZE XL
5. **Pre-harvest:** BIOGREEN POTASH (2 times) + BIOGREEN CALCIUM

### Stage-Wise Application for Cereal Crops (Wheat/Maize)
1. **Seed treatment:** BIOZYME SEED+ at 3-5 ml/kg seed
2. **Basal application:** BIOZYME GRAIN+ GRANULES at 8-16 kg/acre
3. **Vegetative stage:** BIOZYME CROP+ at 250-300 ml/acre + BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS
4. **Flowering stage:** BIOGREEN BORON + AMAZE XL

### Stage-Wise Application for Paddy
1. **Seed/Seedling treatment:** BIOZYME SEED+
2. **Soil application:** BIOZYME POWER+ GRANULES at 6-12 kg/acre
3. **Vegetative stage:** BIOZYME CROP+ 250-300 ml/acre
4. **Panicle initiation:** BIOZYME POWER+ liquid 250-300 ml/acre

---

## BIOSTIMULANT SCIENCE

### Why Seaweed Works
Ascophyllum nodosum contains:
- **Alginic acid / Alginate:** Improves soil water retention; chelates micronutrients
- **Cytokinins:** Promote cell division; delay leaf senescence
- **Auxins:** Root development and elongation
- **Betaines:** Osmoprotectants; drought and salt stress tolerance
- **Mannitol:** Carbon source for soil microorganisms
- **Vitamins & Minerals:** Macro and micro nutrients

### Why Amino Acids Work
L-Amino acids in free form are directly available to plants:
- Building blocks for protein synthesis
- Chelating agents for micronutrients
- Precursors for plant hormones (Auxins, Ethylene)
- Osmoprotectants under stress conditions
- Energy source to improve root growth

# Biostadt Disease Management Guide

This guide provides a comprehensive reference for managing crop diseases using Biostadt fungicides and related products.

## FUNGICIDE CATEGORIES

### Systemic Fungicides (absorbed and translocated within plant)
- **ROKO** (Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP) — Preventive + Curative + Systemic; wide label claims
- **ROKO FLO** (Thiophanate Methyl 41.7% SC) — Liquid version; no dust hazard
- **BIOMYCIN** (Kasugamycin 3% SL) — Antibiotic; fungicidal + bactericidal; no resistance to Paddy blast
- **ALLIANCE** (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) — Dual mode; excellent rain fastness
- **DECIMER** (Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenconazole 11.4% SC) — Phytotonic effect
- **CYGNET** (Myclobutanil 10% WP) — Xylem mobile; vapor activity; 1 hr rainfastness
- **REDEEM** (Difenconazole 25% EC) — Rapid absorption; cost effective
- **TAGMA** (Tebuconazole 25.9% EC) — Prophylactic + Curative + Eradicative
- **TRIGGER PRO** (Hexaconazole 5% SC) — Protective, curative and antisporulant
- **SUPERMATE** (Zineb 68% + Hexaconazole 4% WP) — Zinc nutrition bonus; greening effect
- **RINZEX** (Tebuconazole 6.7% + Captan 26.9% SC) — Acts on spore germination, haustoria, hyphal growth

### Contact Fungicides (protective; remain on leaf surface)
- **BENDACO** (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) — Systemic + Contact; classic proven
- **SPARSH** (Mancozeb 75% WP) — Broad spectrum; provides Zn & Mn; frost protection
- **DOLBY** (Propineb 70% WP) — Multi site; zinc nutrition; no resistance
- **CAPTEK** (Captan 50% WP) — Effective from seedling to maturity; seed borne and soil borne diseases
- **DUOGUARD** (Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) — Rapid penetration (3 hr); against oomycetes
- **SANCHAR** (Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) — Downy mildew specialist
- **WOKOVIT** (Sulphur 80% WG) — Triple action: fungicide + Sulphur nutrient + miticide

---

## DISEASE-SPECIFIC RECOMMENDATIONS

### Powdery Mildew
**Best options:** CYGNET (Myclobutanil), ALLIANCE, TRIGGER PRO, WOKOVIT (Sulphur)
- **Grapes:** CYGNET at 40 gm/10 lit; TRIGGER PRO at 500-1000 ml/ha; WOKOVIT at 1875-2500 gm/ha
- **Apple:** ALLIANCE at 1 ml/lit; RINZEX at 2.5 ml/lit; SUPERMATE at 25 gm/10 lit
- **Wheat:** WOKOVIT at 2500 gm/ha
- **Chilli:** CYGNET at 40 gm/10 lit; RINZEX at 1000 ml/ha; ALLIANCE at 600 ml/ha
- **Mango:** TRIGGER PRO at 1500 ml/ha; WOKOVIT at 1875-2500 gm/ha
- **Cumin:** SANCHAR at 2500 gm/ha

### Downy Mildew
**Best options:** SANCHAR (Metalaxyl + Mancozeb), DUOGUARD (Cymoxanil + Mancozeb), ALLIANCE
- **Grapes:** DUOGUARD at 1500-2000 gm/ha; SANCHAR at 2500 gm/ha; ALLIANCE at 750 ml/ha
- **Potato:** SANCHAR at 2500 gm/ha; DUOGUARD at 1500 gm/ha; BENDACO at 1750 gm/ha
- **Pearl millet:** SANCHAR at 2500 gm/ha
- **Maize:** BENDACO at 1000 gm/ha; DECIMER at 1 ml/lit
- **Cucumber:** DUOGUARD at 1500 gm/ha

### Blast (Rice/Paddy)
**Best options:** BIOMYCIN (no resistance), ROKO, SUPERMATE, SPARSH
- **BIOMYCIN** at 1000-1500 ml/ha — antibiotic; no resistance developed
- **ROKO** at 1000 gm/ha
- **TRIGGER PRO** at 1000 ml/ha
- **SUPERMATE** at 1000-1250 gm/ha
- **SPARSH** at 1.5-2 kg/ha
- **BENDACO** at 750 gm/ha

### Anthracnose
- **Grapes:** ROKO at 715 gm/ha; ALLIANCE at 750 ml/ha; REDEEM at 30 ml/100 lit
- **Chilli:** ROKO FLO at 1000 ml/ha; RINZEX at 1000 ml/ha; DECIMER at 1 ml/lit; ALLIANCE at 600 ml/ha
- **Bottle Gourd:** ROKO at 1070-1430 gm/ha

### Scab (Apple)
**Best options:** CAPTEK, CYGNET, RINZEX, DOLBY, TRIGGER PRO
- **CAPTEK** at 2500 gm/ha
- **CYGNET** at 40 gm/10 lit water
- **RINZEX** at 1550 ml/ha or 2.5 ml/lit water
- **DOLBY** at 300 gm/100 lit water
- **TRIGGER PRO** at 50 ml/100 lit
- **ALLIANCE** at 1 ml/lit; **ROKO** at 715 gm/ha; **WOKOVIT** at 1875-2500 gm/ha

### Sheath Blight (Rice)
- **ALLIANCE** at 750 ml/ha
- **DECIMER** at 1 ml/lit
- **REDEEM** at 500 ml/ha
- **TAGMA** at 750 ml/ha
- **TRIGGER PRO** at 1000 ml/ha

### Fusarium Wilt
- **Pigeon Pea:** ROKO at 1430 gm/ha

### Purple Blotch (Onion)
- **ALLIANCE** at 750 ml/ha
- **DECIMER** at 500 ml/ha
- **TAGMA** at 625-750 ml/ha
- **REDEEM** at 500 ml/ha

### Fruit Rot / Dieback (Chilli)
- **ROKO FLO** at 1000 ml/ha
- **ALLIANCE** at 600 ml/ha
- **RINZEX** at 1000 ml/ha
- **REDEEM** at 50 ml/100 lit
- **DOLBY** at 500 gm/ha

### Late Blight (Potato/Tomato)
- **SANCHAR** at 2500 gm/ha
- **DUOGUARD** at 1500 gm/ha
- **CAPTEK** at 2500 gm/ha
- **ALLIANCE** at 750 ml/ha
- **DOLBY** at 300 gm/ha

### Brown Rust / Yellow Rust (Wheat)
- **ALLIANCE** at 750 ml/ha
- **DECIMER** at 1 ml/lit
- **ROKO** at 715 gm/ha
- **SPARSH** at 1.5-2 kg/ha

---

## RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT PRINCIPLES

1. **Rotate fungicide classes:** Alternate between SDHI, DMI, QoI, multi-site, and contact fungicides
2. **Use combinations:** Products with dual mode of action (ALLIANCE, DECIMER, BENDACO) reduce resistance risk
3. **BIOMYCIN (Kasugamycin):** No resistance has developed against Paddy blast — preserve its efficacy by not overusing
4. **SPARSH, DOLBY (Mancozeb-based):** Multi-site action; very low resistance risk; ideal for resistance management
5. **HITONE (Immunity booster):** Use as part of program to reduce total fungicide applications

---

## FORMULATION GUIDE

| Formulation | Full Name | Advantage |
|---|---|---|
| WP | Wettable Powder | Easy to mix; cost effective |
| SC | Suspension Concentrate | Better leaf coverage; no dust |
| EC | Emulsifiable Concentrate | Penetrates waxy surfaces well |
| WG/WDG | Water Dispersible Granule | No dust; accurate measurement |
| SL | Soluble Liquid | Fast absorption |

---

## COMPATIBILITY NOTE
- Always add **4 IN 1 Sticker** (500-700 ml/ha) to spray tank to improve sticking, spreading, and rain fastness
- Mix fungicides with **BIOGREEN** micronutrients for combined disease control and nutrition
- **HITONE** (Cinnamon Oil based) can be tankmixed with most fungicides as a plant immunity booster

# Biostadt Herbicide Guide

Complete reference for Biostadt herbicides for weed management across major crops.

---

## HERBICIDE CLASSIFICATION

### By Selectivity
| Type | Products | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Selective | MAACHIS, PATINA, NANCHAKU, NANCHAKU POWER, HITBAC, RIMOVE PLUS, REMEDUO, DESTAL XTRA | Safe on target crop; kills specific weeds |
| Non-selective | BRAKE (Glyphosate 41%), BRAKE G (Glyphosate 71%), PARQ (Paraquat) | Kills all vegetation; use with care |

### By Application Timing
| Timing | Products |
|---|---|
| Pre-emergence | HITBAC (Pendimethalin), RIMOVE PLUS (Pretilachlor) |
| Post-emergence (selective) | MAACHIS, PATINA, NANCHAKU, NANCHAKU POWER, REMEDUO, DESTAL XTRA |
| Non-selective (pre/post) | BRAKE, BRAKE G, PARQ |
| Pre + Early Post | NANCHAKU POWER |

---

## CROP-SPECIFIC WEED MANAGEMENT

### Wheat
**Key weeds:** Phalaris minor (Canary grass), Wild oat (Avena sp.)

| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Weeds Controlled |
|---|---|---|---|
| MAACHIS | Clodinafop-propargyl 15% WP | 400 gm/ha | Phalaris minor only |
| PATINA | Pinoxaden 5.1% EC | 800-900 ml/ha | Phalaris minor + Wild oat |

**Decision guide:**
- **MAACHIS** if only Phalaris minor (Canary grass) is the problem
- **PATINA** if both Phalaris minor AND Wild oat (Avena sp.) are present; also has better rain fastness (60 min)
- Both have wide window of application and superior crop tolerance

### Rice (Transplanted)
**Key weeds:** Echinochloa spp., Cyperus spp., Digitaria sanguinalis

| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Timing |
|---|---|---|---|
| RIMOVE PLUS | Pretilachlor 37% EW | 1500-1875 ml/ha | Pre-emergence |

**Notes:** Apply within 3-5 days of transplanting; can be used on any rice variety; no phytotoxicity on paddy

### Cotton
**Key weeds:** Trianthema spp., Amaranthus, Digitaria, Celosia

| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Weeds | Timing |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NANCHAKU | Pyrithiobac Sodium 10% EC | 625-750 ml/ha | Broad-leaf only | Post-emergence |
| NANCHAKU POWER | Pyrithiobac 6% + Quizalofop 4% MEC | 1000-1250 ml/ha | Broad-leaf + Grasses | Pre + early post |
| HITBAC | Pendimethalin 38.7% CS | 1500-1700 ml/ha | Annual grasses, sedges, broadleaf | Pre-emergence |

**Decision guide:**
- **NANCHAKU:** When only broad-leaf weeds are present
- **NANCHAKU POWER:** One-shot solution for both grass and broad-leaf weeds; no tank-mixing needed
- **HITBAC:** Use as pre-emergence to prevent weed germination

### Soybean
| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Timing |
|---|---|---|---|
| HITBAC | Pendimethalin 38.7% CS | 1500-1750 ml/ha | Pre-emergence |
| REMEDUO | Fomesafen 11.1% + Fluazifop-P-Butyl 11.1% SL | 1000 ml/ha | Post-emergence |

**REMEDUO** controls both grass and broad-leaf weeds post-emergence; fast acting; no residual

### Groundnut
| Product | Dose | Timing |
|---|---|---|
| REMEDUO | 1000 ml/ha | Post-emergence |

### Maize
| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Timing |
|---|---|---|---|
| DESTAL XTRA | Mesotrione 2.27% + Atrazine 22.7% SC | 3500 ml/ha | Post-emergence |

Unique HPPD + PS II chemistry; in-built adjuvant; controls grasses and broad-leaf weeds

### Sugarcane
| Product | Dose | Weeds |
|---|---|---|
| DESTAL XTRA | 3500 ml/ha | Trianthema, Amaranthus, Echinochloa, Cyperus rotundus |

### Tea & Plantation Crops
| Product | Active Ingredient | Dose | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAKE | Glyphosate 41% SL | 2000-3000 ml/ha | Non-selective systemic |
| BRAKE G | Glyphosate 71% SG | 3000 gm/ha | Non-selective systemic |
| PARQ | Paraquat 24% SL | 2000-3000 ml/ha | Non-selective contact |

**Difference between BRAKE and PARQ:**
- **BRAKE (Glyphosate):** Systemic; absorbed by foliage and translocated to roots; kills from roots up; takes 7-12 days; effective on perennials
- **PARQ (Paraquat):** Contact only; fast knockdown of green tissue; biologically inactive in soil; annual weeds

---

## PRODUCT PROFILES

### HITBAC (Pendimethalin 38.7% CS)
- Forms a herbicide barrier at soil surface preventing weed germination
- Residual control kills weeds as they emerge
- **No residual effect on subsequent/follow crop** — safe crop rotation
- Crops: Soybean, Cotton, Onion, Chilli, Groundnut, Mustard, Cumin

### MAACHIS (Clodinafop-propargyl 15% WP)
- Inhibits ACCase enzyme in grass weeds
- Active growth ceases within **48 hours** of application
- Wide window of application — flexibility for time of application
- Gives control even under unfavorable climatic conditions

### PATINA (Pinoxaden 5.1% EC)
- Quickly translocates within plant acropetally & basipetally
- Good rain fastness — **60 minutes**
- Weed growth stops immediately
- Superior control across broad spectrum of grass weeds
- Safe to Wheat crop and subsequent crops

### NANCHAKU POWER (Pyrithiobac 6% + Quizalofop 4% MEC)
- ME (Micro-Emulsion) formulation — thermodynamically stable; improves bioavailability
- Controls BOTH narrow and broad leaf weeds in ONE application
- No tank mixing required
- Safe to cotton at any stage of growth

### DESTAL XTRA (Mesotrione 2.27% + Atrazine 22.7% SC)
- Unique HPPD + PS II chemistry
- In-built adjuvant — no separate mixing needed
- Ready-mix combination for better weed control than single molecules

### BRAKE (Glyphosate 41% SL)
- Non-selective systemic — translocates through xylem to roots and rhizomes
- Kills perennial weeds from roots
- Does not affect germination of ensuing crops
- Any crop can be grown after its application

### REMEDUO (Fomesafen + Fluazifop-P-Butyl)
- Fomesafen: PPO inhibitor — controls broad-leaf weeds
- Fluazifop-P-Butyl: ACCase inhibitor — controls grass weeds
- Combined: Controls BOTH grass and broad-leaf weeds in one application
- Fast acting; no residual effect

---

## HERBICIDE SAFETY PRECAUTIONS

1. **BRAKE / PARQ (non-selective):** Never allow spray to drift onto crop leaves — will kill the crop
2. **DESTAL XTRA (Atrazine-based):** Observe soil persistence; avoid in rotation with sensitive crops
3. **RIMOVE PLUS:** Apply within recommended window after transplanting; do not apply during active growth stages
4. **Add 4 IN 1 Sticker** to herbicide sprays for better adhesion and rain fastness
5. Always spray when weeds are young and actively growing for best results
6. Calibrate sprayer accurately — over-dosing may damage crop, under-dosing may fail to control weeds

# Biostadt Pest Management Guide

Comprehensive reference for managing insect pests using Biostadt insecticides.

---

## INSECTICIDE CATEGORIES BY MODE OF ACTION

### Neonicotinoids (Systemic — sucking pests)
- **ULTIMO** (Imidacloprid 17.8% SL) — High selectivity; seed treatment option
- **ULTIMO SUPER** (Imidaclopride 30.5% SC) — Enhanced SC formulation; phytotonic effect
- **EDAN** (Imidacloprid 70% WG) — Phytotonic effect; vigorous plant growth
- **EVIDENT** (Thiamethoxam 25% WG) — Granular; soil drench option; paddy nursery
- **EVIDENT FLO** (Thiamethoxam 30% FS) — Seed treatment formulation
- **WAPKIL** (Acetamiprid 20% SP) — Triple action: ovicidal, adulticidal, larvicidal

### Pyrethroids (Contact & Stomach — broad spectrum)
- **AUZAR** (Cypermethrin 25% EC) — Lepidopteran pests; longer persistence
- **KLINTOP** (Bifenthrin 10% EC) — Phyto-tonic effect; mites + insects
- **STOP** (Alphamethrin 10% EC) — Quick knockdown; ovicidal action

### Phenylpyrazoles / Fipronil (Soil & Foliar — broad spectrum)
- **JAANBAZ SC** (Fipronil 5% SC) — Contact + stomach + systemic
- **JAANBAZ GR** (Fipronil 0.3% GR) — Soil application; termites, borers
- **JAANBAZ POWER** (Thiamethoxam 0.9% + Fipronil 0.2% WG) — Dual nervous system action

### Diamides (Lepidopteran specialists)
- **TREMOR** (Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC) — Fast cessation of feeding; long duration; IPM safe
- **FULSTOP-D** (Flubendiamide 20% WG) — All stages of caterpillars; RRM technology

### Avermectins (Trans-laminar — Lepidoptera + mites)
- **BIOCLAIM** (Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG) — Naturally occurring; trans-laminar
- **BIOCLAIM PRO** (Emamectin Benzoate 1.5% + Profenophos 35% WDG) — Dual mode; 24-48 hr kill

### Acaricides (Mite specialists)
- **MAIDEN** (Hexythiazox 5.45% EC) — First IGR Acaricide in India; ovicidal + larvicidal + nymphicidal; no phytotoxicity

### Others
- **DERBY** (Diafenthiuron 50% WP) — Vapor action; sucking complex + mites; phytotonic

- **KRUSH** (Quinolphos 25% EC) — Broad spectrum; contact + stomach
- **ULKA 505** (Chlorpyriphos 50% + Cypermethrin 5% EC) — Broad spectrum; rain resistant
- **STRIKE** (Dinotefuran 15% + Pymetrozine 45% WG) — BPH specialist; SPB technology

## IPM PRODUCT SELECTION GUIDE

| Pest Type | IPM-Preferred Products | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Caterpillars/Borers | TREMOR, FULSTOP-D, BIOCLAIM | Safer to beneficial insects |
| Sucking pests | EVIDENT, ULTIMO, WAPKIL | Selective; low impact on natural enemies |
| Mites | MAIDEN | No adverse effect on beneficial insects; no cross resistance |
| Soil pests | JAANBAZ GR, JAANBAZ POWER | Targeted soil application |

## RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT TIPS
1. Rotate between different classes: Neonicotinoids → Pyrethroids → Diamides → Avermectins
2. TREMOR and FULSTOP-D: no cross resistance; preserve efficacy by rotation
3. MAIDEN: no cross resistance to mites tolerant to conventional acaricides
4. STRIKE uses unique SPB technology — no known resistance in BPH
5. Combine chemical control with BIOZYME biostimulants to build plant vigor and natural resistance

# Wheat Pest Management (India)

## Aphids (Rhopalosiphum padi)
- Typical signs: curling leaves, sticky honeydew, black sooty mold.
- Risk window: late vegetative to grain filling when weather is mild.
- Threshold idea: check 20 tillers at 5 spots before spray decisions.

## Integrated Management
1. Field scouting twice per week in early morning.
2. Avoid excess nitrogen in single heavy dose.
3. Keep border weeds under control to reduce alternate hosts.
4. Use yellow sticky traps for monitoring.

## Chemical Use Safety
- Rotate mode of action to delay resistance.
- Spray in low wind periods.
- Respect pre-harvest interval on product label.
- Follow local agricultural extension guidance.
irrigationScheduleWheat:
# Wheat Irrigation Scheduling Guide

## Critical Growth Stages
1. Crown root initiation (CRI): around 20-25 days after sowing.
2. Tillering.
3. Late jointing / booting.
4. Flowering.
5. Milking / dough stage.

## Practical Water Advice
- CRI irrigation is highest priority for yield.
- Use field moisture checks before each irrigation.
- Avoid waterlogging to prevent root stress.
- In sandy soils, prefer lighter and more frequent irrigation.

## Low-Cost Moisture Check
- Take soil from 10-15 cm depth.
- If soil does not form a weak ball in hand, irrigation is likely needed.
- Re-check in multiple field spots before decision.

cropRecommendations.guidance: For Phalaris minor (Canary grass) in wheat, use MAACHIS (Clodinafop-propargyl 15% WP) at 400 gm/ha or PATINA (Pinoxaden 5.1% EC) at 800-900 ml/ha as post emergent selective herbicide. Both have wide window of application. PATINA offers rain fastness of 60 min and is safe for subsequent crops.
For Wild oat (Avena sp.) in wheat, PATINA (Pinoxaden 5.1% EC) at 800-900 ml/ha is the recommended post emergent herbicide. It controls both Phalaris minor and Avena ludoviciana effectively.

For Brown rust and Yellow Rust in wheat, use ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 750 ml/ha or DECIMER (Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenconazole 11.4% SC) at 1 ml/lit. ROKO (Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP) at 715 gm/ha controls Brown rust & leaf Blight. SPARSH (Mancozeb 75% WP) at 1.5-2 kg/ha covers Brown & black rust, Blight.
For aphids in wheat, use EVIDENT (Thiamethoxam 25% WG) at 50 gm/ha or KRUSH (Quinolphos 25% EC) at 1000-1600 gm/ha. KRUSH also controls mites in wheat.
For termites in wheat, use EVIDENT FLO (Thiamethoxam 30% FS) at 3.3 ml per kg seed as seed treatment, or JAANBAZ GR (Fipronil 0.3% GR) at 20 kg/ha.

For wheat, use BIOZYME GRAIN+ GRANULES at 8-16 kg/acre as soil application to enhance number of productive tillers, improve photosynthesis, and develop tolerance to biotic & abiotic stress. AMAZE X at 4-8 kg/acre is also effective for all grain crops.
For Powdery Mildew in wheat, use WOKOVIT (Sulphur 80% WG) at 2500 gm/ha. It also provides Sulphur micronutrient and has additional miticide action.

For Paddy Blast, BIOMYCIN (Kasugamycin 3% SL) at 1000-1500 ml/ha is the recommended antibiotic fungicide with no resistance development. ROKO (Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP) at 1000 gm/ha, TRIGGER PRO (Hexaconazole 5% SC) at 1000 ml/ha, SPARSH (Mancozeb 75% WP) at 1.5-2 kg/ha, and BENDACO (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) at 750 gm/ha are also effective.

For Sheath Blight in rice/paddy, use ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 750 ml/ha, DECIMER at 1 ml/lit, REDEEM (Difenconazole 25% EC) at 500 ml/ha, TAGMA (Tebuconazole 25.9% EC) at 750 ml/ha, or TRIGGER PRO at 1000 ml/ha.

For Stem Borer and Leaf folder in rice, use TREMOR (Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC) at 150 ml/ha (long duration control), FULSTOP-D (Flubendiamide 20% WG) at 125 gm/ha, JAANBAZ SC (Fipronil 5% SC) at 1000-1500 ml/ha, KLINTOP (Bifenthrin 10% EC) at 500 ml/ha, or DARTRIZ SP (Cartap Hydrochloride 50% SP) at 1 kg/ha.

For Brown Plant Hopper (BPH), STRIKE (Dinotefuran 15% + Pymetrozine 45% WG) at 33 gm/ha is the most effective - it makes BPH IMMOBILE and stops reproduction. ULTIMO (Imidacloprid 17.8% SL) at 100-125 ml/ha, ULTIMO SUPER (Imidaclopride 30.5% SC) at 60-75 ml/ha, EVIDENT (Thiamethoxam 25% WG) at 100 gm/ha, and WAPKIL (Acetamiprid 20% SP) at 50-100 gm/ha are also effective.

For paddy, use BIOZYME POWER+ GRANULES at 6-12 kg/acre for enhancing productive tillers and uniform panicle emergence. As foliar spray, BIOZYME POWER+ liquid at 250-300 ml/acre improves panicle emergence and seed weight.

For weed management in transplanted rice, use RIMOVE PLUS (Pretilachlor 37% EW) at 1500-1875 ml/ha as pre-emergence herbicide. It controls major grass weeds including Echinochloa species and sedges like Cyperus iria.

For Bollworm complex in cotton, use TREMOR (Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC) at 150 ml/ha, FULSTOP-D (Flubendiamide 20% WG) at 250 gm/ha, PRAHAR-C (Profenophos 40% EC + Cypermethrin 4% EC) at 1000-1500 gm/ha, AUZAR (Cypermethrin 25% EC) at 160-280 ml/ha, or BIOCLAIM (Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG) at 190-220 gm/ha.

For sucking pests (Whitefly, Jassid, Thrips, Aphids) in cotton, use BIOCLAIM PRO (Emamectin Benzoate 1.5% + Profenophos 35% WDG) at 700 gm/ha, DERBY (Diafenthiuron 50% WP) at 600 gm/ha, EDAN (Imidacloprid 70% WG) at 30-35 gm/ha, ULTIMO (Imidacloprid 17.8% SL) at 100-125 ml/ha, or WAPKIL (Acetamiprid 20% SP) at 50-100 gm/ha.

For weed management in cotton, use NANCHAKU (Pyrithiobac Sodium 10% EC) at 625-750 ml/ha for broad-leaf weeds only, or NANCHAKU POWER (Pyrithiobac Sodium 6% + Quizalofop ethyl 4% MEC) at 1000-1250 ml/ha for both broad-leaf and grass weeds. HITBAC (Pendimethalin 38.7% CS) at 1500-1700 ml/ha is used as pre-emergence herbicide.
For BT Cotton, use BIOZYME BT+ at 250-300 ml/acre as foliar spray to improve boll development, boll size, and lint quality. Apply during flowering and boll development stages.

For soil pests (White Grub) in groundnut or cotton, use JAANBAZ POWER (Thiamethoxam 0.9% + Fipronil 0.2% WG) at 12-15 kg/ha as soil application. For termites, KLINTOP (Bifenthrin 10% EC) and JAANBAZ SC (Fipronil 5% SC) are effective.

For Apple Scab, use CYGNET (Myclobutanil 10% WP) at 40 gm/10 lit water, RINZEX (Tebuconazole 6.7% + Captan 26.9% SC) at 2.5 ml/lit water, TRIGGER PRO (Hexaconazole 5% SC) at 50 ml/100 lit, CAPTEK (Captan 50% WP) at 2500 gm/ha, ROKO (Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP) at 715 gm/ha, ALLIANCE at 1 ml/lit, or DOLBY at 300 gm/100 lit water.
For Powdery Mildew in apple, use ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 1 ml/lit water, RINZEX at 2.5 ml/lit, or SUPERMATE (Zineb 68% + Hexaconazole 4% WP) at 25 gm/10 lit water.
For European mites in apple, use MAIDEN (Hexythiazox 5.45% EC) at 4 ml per lit water. This is the first IGR acaricide in India with excellent ovicidal, larvicidal, and nymphicidal activity.
For Wooly Aphids in apple, use KRUSH (Quinolphos 25% EC) at 3000-4000 gm/ha.

For apple, use BIOZYME FRUIT+ GRANULES at 750 gm per plant for better root development and bud stimulation. Use BIOZYME FRUIT+ liquid at 1.5-2 ml/lit water as foliar spray during fruit development to improve fruit size, color, and quality.

For apple, apply BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS at 0.5-1 ml/lit at 30-35 and 45-50 days after pruning/bahar treatment to prevent zinc deficiency. Apply BIOGREEN BORON at 1-1.5 ml/lit at pre-flowering and fruit setting stage for pollination and seed setting. Apply BIOGREEN CALCIUM at 2-3 ml/lit at 60, 40, and 20 days before harvest to prevent fruit disorders.

For Downy Mildew in grapes, use DUOGUARD (Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) at 1500-2000 gm/ha, SANCHAR (Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) at 2500 gm/ha, BENDACO (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) at 0.15%, DOLBY (Propineb 70% WP) at 300 gm/100 lit, or ALLIANCE at 750 ml/ha.

For Powdery Mildew in grapes, use CYGNET (Myclobutanil 10% WP) at 40 gm/10 lit water, TRIGGER PRO (Hexaconazole 5% SC) at 500-1000 ml/ha, WOKOVIT (Sulphur 80% WG) at 1875-2500 gm/ha, ALLIANCE at 750 ml/ha, or REDEEM at 30 ml/100 lit water.

For berry size improvement in grapes, use SITOFEX (Forchlorfenuron 0.12% EC) at 2 ml/ltr at 3-4 mm berry size stage and 1-2 ml/ltr at 6-8 mm berry size stage. For dormancy breaking, use DORMEX (Hydrogen Cyanamide 49% AS) at 30-40 ml/ltr.
For Red spider mites in grapes, use MAIDEN (Hexythiazox 5.45% EC) at 500 ml/ha.

For Early Shoot Borer and Top Borer in sugarcane, use TREMOR (Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC) at 375-625 ml/ha, FULSTOP-D (Flubendiamide 20% WG) at 375 gm/ha, or JAANBAZ GR (Fipronil 0.3% GR) at 25-33.3 kg/ha.
For Termites in sugarcane, use KLINTOP (Bifenthrin 10% EC) at 1000 ml/ha or ULTIMO (Imidacloprid 17.8% SL) at 350 ml/ha.
For sugarcane, use BIOZYME BIOCANE+ GRANULES at 8-16 kg/acre for soil application to enhance tillering, bud stimulation, cane girth, and inter nodal length. This leads to higher yield.
For weed management in sugarcane, use DESTAL XTRA (Mesotrione 2.27% + Atrazine 22.7% SC) at 3500 ml/ha as post emergent selective herbicide to control grasses and broad leaf weeds.

For Early Blight and Late Blight in tomato, use DECIMER (Azoxystrobin 18.2% + Difenconazole 11.4% SC) at 1 ml/lit, ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 750 ml/ha, CAPTEK (Captan 50% WP) at 2500 gm/ha, BENDACO at 0.25%, BIOMYCIN (Kasugamycin 3% SL) at 1000-1500 ml/ha, or SPARSH (Mancozeb 75% WP) at 1.5-2 kg/ha.

For Fruit borer in tomato, use TREMOR (Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC) at 150 ml/ha, FULSTOP-D (Flubendiamide 20% WG) at 250 gm/ha, BIOCLAIM (Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG) at 200 gm/ha, or KRUSH (Quinolphos 25% EC) at 1000 gm/ha.
For Whitefly in tomato, use ULTIMO (Imidacloprid 17.8% SL) at 15-175 ml/ha, DERBY (Diafenthiuron 50% WP) at 600 gm/ha, or EVIDENT (Thiamethoxam 25% WG) at 200 gm/ha.

For Anthracnose and Dieback in chilli, use ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 600 ml/ha, ROKO FLO (Thiophanate Methyl 41.7% SC) at 1000 ml/ha, BENDACO at 750 gm/ha, REDEEM (Difenconazole 25% EC) at 50 ml/100 lit, RINZEX at 1000 ml/ha, DECIMER at 1 ml/lit, CYGNET at 40 gm/10 lit, or DOLBY at 500 gm/ha.

For Yellow mites and mites in chilli, use MAIDEN (Hexythiazox 5.45% EC) at 300-500 ml/ha, BIOCLAIM PRO at 500 gm/ha, or DERBY (Diafenthiuron 50% WP) at 600 gm/ha. KRUSH (Quinolphos 25% EC) at 1000-1500 gm/ha also controls mites.

For chilli, use BIOZYME MIRCHI+ at 250-300 ml/acre as foliar spray to increase branching, improve flowering and fruit formation, enhance fruit size and shining, keep rhizosphere active, and improve food translocation from leaves to fruits.

For Late Blight and Early Blight in potato, use SANCHAR (Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) at 2500 gm/ha, DUOGUARD (Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP) at 1500 gm/ha, BENDACO (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) at 1750 gm/ha, CAPTEK (Captan 50% WP) at 2500 gm/ha, DOLBY at 300 gm/ha, ALLIANCE at 750 ml/ha, or SPARSH (Mancozeb 75% WP) at 1.5-2 kg/ha.
For Grain Discoloration in paddy, use ROKO (Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP) at 1000 gm/ha or SUPERMATE (Zineb 68% + Hexaconazole 4% WP) at 1000-1250 gm/ha.

For Purple blotch in onion, use ALLIANCE (Azoxystrobin 11% + Tebuconazole 18.3% SC) at 750 ml/ha, DECIMER at 500 ml/ha, TAGMA (Tebuconazole 25.9% EC) at 625-750 ml/ha, or REDEEM (Difenconazole 25% EC) at 500 ml/ha.

For weed management in soybean, use REMEDUO (Fomesafen 11.1% + Fluazifop-P-Butyl 11.1% SL) at 1000 ml/ha as post emergent selective herbicide to control both grasses and broad-leaf weeds. HITBAC (Pendimethalin 38.7% CS) at 1500-1750 ml/ha is used as pre-emergence herbicide.
For soybean and other pulse/oilseed crops, use BIOZYME PULSE+ at 250-300 ml/acre as foliar spray to improve flowering, pod formation, pod size, grain shining, and yield.
For Fall Army worms in maize, use BIOCLAIM PRO (Emamectin Benzoate 1.5% + Profenophos 35% WDG) at 500 gm/ha. This provides dual mode of action with ovi-larvicidal control.
For weed management in maize, use DESTAL XTRA (Mesotrione 2.27% + Atrazine 22.7% SC) at 3500 ml/ha as post emergent selective herbicide.
For Scarlet Mite and Red Spider Mite in tea, use MAIDEN (Hexythiazox 5.45% EC) at 300-500 ml/ha.
For Tea diseases (Blister blight, grey blight, black rot), use BENDACO (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) at 1250 gm/ha or SUPERMATE (Zineb 68% + Hexaconazole 4% WP) at 625 gm/ha.
For tea, use BIOZYME TEA+ at 500 ml/hectare as foliar spray. It reduces plucking interval, increases chlorophyll formation, helps bud stimulation, and improves leaf quality & yield.

For weed management in tea, use BRAKE (Glyphosate 41% SL) at 2000-3000 ml/ha as non-selective systemic herbicide for perennial grasses and broad-leaf weeds. PARQ (Paraquat Dichloride 24% SL) at 2000-3000 ml/ha is used as non-selective contact herbicide.

For Tikka leaf spot and Rust in groundnut, use BENDACO (Carbendazim 12% + Mancozeb 63% WP) at 500 gm/ha, TRIGGER PRO (Hexaconazole 5% SC) at 1000 ml/ha, TAGMA (Tebuconazole 25.9% EC) at 500-750 ml/ha, REDEEM at 100 ml/100 lit, or SPARSH (Mancozeb 75% WP) at 1.5-2 kg/ha.
For White Grub in groundnut, use JAANBAZ POWER (Thiamethoxam 0.9% + Fipronil 0.2% WG) at 12-15 kg/ha as soil application.

For Zinc deficiency in crops, use BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS (Zinc Oxide 39.5%) at 0.5-1 ml/lit as foliar spray or BIOGREEN ZINC (Zinc EDTA 12%) at 1-2 gm/liter as foliar spray or 2.5-5 kg/ha as soil application. Zinc is essential as catalyst for enzymes in photosynthesis and improves immunity.

For Boron deficiency affecting pollination and seed setting, use BIOGREEN BORON (Boron Ethanolamine 10%) at 1-1.5 ml/lit water at pre-flowering and fruit setting stages. For combined Calcium & Boron deficiency, use BIOGREEN CALCIUM B PLUS (Ca 14% + B 6.9% + N 4%) at 1 ml/lit.

For Calcium deficiency causing poor fruit quality and fruit disorders, use BIOGREEN CALCIUM (Concentrated Liquid Calcium 11%) at 2-3 ml/lit water applied at 60, 40, and 20 days before harvest for fruit crops. It strengthens cell walls and manages stress.

For improving fruit quality, uniform size, color and to speed up ripening, apply BIOGREEN POTASH (Concentrated Liquid Potassium 46.5%) at 2-2.5 ml/lit water. Apply to fruit crops 30 and 15 days before harvesting. It minimizes fruit disorders and maximizes crop productivity.

For improving seed germination and early plant establishment in all crops, use BIOZYME SEED+ (Seaweed + L-Amino acid 6%) at 3-5 ml per kg seed. For seedling treatment, use 150-200 ml per acre seedling. It provides quick & uniform germination and reduces transplanting shock.

For crops under drip irrigation, use BIOZYME DRIP+ (Seaweed + Humic Acid 4% + Amino acids 4%) at 2.5 Lit/Hectare. It promotes white root feeder development, improves CN ratio of soil, and enhances nutrient availability by soil pH balance.

MOBILIZER (Arbuscular mycorrhiza fungus) extends root absorption area by more than 700 times, allowing roots to access water and nutrients beyond the root zone. It improves soil health, increases stress tolerance, and improves water utilization. Apply to soil at time of planting.

For Integrated Pest Management (IPM), preferred products are: HITONE (Cinnamon Oil based immunity booster) - zero PHI, safe till harvesting; BIOCLAIM (Emamectin Benzoate) - safer to beneficial insects; TREMOR (Chlorantraniliprole) - safer to beneficial insects; EVIDENT/ULTIMO (Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam) - selective on sucking pests; MAIDEN (Hexythiazox) - no adverse effect on beneficial insects. Always combine chemical control with biostimulants for sustainable farming.

Add 4 IN 1 (Non Silicon Sticker) at 500-700 ml/ha to any spray mixture of insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, or foliar fertilizers to improve efficacy. It sticks, spreads uniformly, activates effectiveness, reduces spray drift, and has rain fastness property for use in high rainfall areas.

For Iron deficiency causing chlorosis and poor photosynthesis, use BIOGREEN IRON (Ferrous EDTA 12%) at 1-1.5 gm/liter for foliar spray or 2.5-5 kg/ha as soil/drip application. Iron is essential for chlorophyll production, nitrogen fixation, and protein synthesis.

For vegetable crops, use BIOZYME VEGETABLES+ GRANULES at 8-16 kg/acre as soil application for root development and stress tolerance. Use BIOZYME VEGETABLE+ liquid at 250-300 ml/acre as foliar spray for better fruit setting, fruit size, and quality. AMAZE XL at 1-2 ml/lit is ideal during flower/fruit development stage.

For premium fruit quality, follow this nutrition program: 1) Apply BIOGREEN ZINC PLUS at 0.5-1 ml/lit after pruning for enzyme activity; 2) Apply BIOGREEN BORON at 1-1.5 ml/lit at pre-flowering for pollination; 3) Apply BIOGREEN CALCIUM B PLUS at 1 ml/lit at fruit setting stage for cell strengthening; 4) Apply BIOGREEN CALCIUM at 2-3 ml/lit at 60, 40, 20 days before harvest for fruit quality; 5) Apply BIOGREEN POTASH at 2-2.5 ml/lit at 30 and 15 days before harvest for uniform color, size and faster ripening.
